microsoftSourceClaims.claimText: As of May 1, 2026, Microsoft Agent 365 is generally available for the Commercial segment on a per user basis and provides a centralized agent registry and lifecycle-governance control plane.
The agent registry is reached in the Microsoft 365 admin center by selecting Agents > All Agents > Registry, which lists the agents available to the organization.

A Copilot Studio agent is published from a draft; each update is published again and applies to connected channels. No documented canonical rollback event API was identified on the cited publishing and ALM surfaces as of 2026-07-14, so rollback references remain customer-supplied solution/change evidence.

Copilot Studio agents are moved between environments through Power Platform solutions. Development commonly uses unmanaged solutions and downstream environments use managed solutions; environment variables and connection references parameterize environment-specific values, and agents must be published in the target environment before changes are available.
The Power Apps and Power Automate admin modules require Windows PowerShell 5.x; they use .NET Framework, which is incompatible with PowerShell 6.0 and later.
Power Platform API uses delegated permissions only at this time; for service principal identities, assign a scoped RBAC role such as Reader or Contributor instead of application permissions.

licenses: Microsoft Copilot Studio license assigned to the test operator
Qualifying Microsoft Agent 365 license for at least one user where registry inspection is in scope
roles: AI Reader for read-only Agent 365 registry inspection
Power Platform environment user with a custom read-only Dataverse security role for solution and agent metadata
features: Copilot Studio enabled in a non-production Power Platform environment
Microsoft Agent 365 registry available where licensed
capacityRequirements: One non-production Power Platform environment for representative sample validation
dataRequirements: Representative sample records only; no real agent IDs, environment IDs, solution unique names, owner identities, connection references, secrets, or customer content are retained in committed lab evidence

steps.intent: Confirm the approved lab tenant before any Agent 365 or Power Platform inspection.
steps.expected: The organization id and displayName match the separately maintained sanctioned-lab authorization record.
steps.operatorNote: Compare the response with the out-of-band lab authorization record and stop on any mismatch.

readBack.reference: Microsoft Graph organization response and sanctioned-lab record
readBack.expectation: Tenant identity matches the separately maintained sanctioned-lab record.

steps.intent: Confirm the non-production Power Platform environment and operator read scope before inspection.
steps.expected: The target environment is visible and the operator has read-only access.
steps.locator: Power Platform admin center > Environments > (target environment) > Access

readBack.expectation: Environment details confirm read-only operator access without granting new roles.

phases.title: Inspect registry, agent lifecycle metadata, and run sample scripts

steps.intent: Inspect the Microsoft Agent 365 registry aggregate to observe agent inventory context where licensed.
steps.expected: The registry lists agents available to the organization; no availability or installation settings are changed.
steps.locator: Microsoft 365 admin center > Agents > All Agents > Registry

readBack.expectation: Registry summary displays agent counts and inventory without any change to configuration.

steps.intent: Inspect a Copilot Studio agent's draft versus published state without publishing.
steps.expected: Draft and published state are visible; no publish or republish action is performed.
steps.locator: Copilot Studio (copilotstudio.microsoft.com) > target environment > Agents > (agent) > Overview / Publish

readBack.reference: Agent publish state and solution metadata
readBack.expectation: Published/draft state is recorded read only, with no lifecycle mutation.

steps.intent: Inspect the Power Platform solution containing the agent and record managed/unmanaged state, solution version, environment-variable definitions, and connection-reference presence without export or import.
steps.expected: Solution membership and ALM metadata are visible in the Power Apps maker portal; no solution export, import, upgrade, or publish occurs.
steps.locator: Power Apps maker portal (make.powerapps.com) > target environment > Solutions > (agent solution)

readBack.expectation: Managed/unmanaged state, solution version, agent membership, environment-variable definitions, and connection-reference presence are recorded read-only with identifiers pseudonymized.

steps.intent: Run the documentation-first monitoring script to produce a representative lifecycle snapshot from the local stub.
steps.expected: Script emits a stub warning and writes a representative monitoring snapshot without contacting live services.

readBack.expectation: Output includes the local-cslt-stub source marker and representative inventory, approval, and review counts.

steps.intent: Run the documentation-first evidence export and confirm SHA-256 companions are produced.
steps.expected: Four JSON artifacts and matching .sha256 companion files are written for the selected tier.

readBack.expectation: Each evidence JSON file has a matching .sha256 companion and the package validates against the shared schema.

phases.title: Verify source citations and security posture

steps.intent: Confirm each Microsoft source claim resolves on Microsoft Learn and matches the documented lifecycle state.
steps.expected: All cited Microsoft Learn URLs remain published and consistent with the documented generally-available lifecycle state.
steps.operatorNote: Confirmed each microsoftSourceClaims.sourceUrl resolves on Microsoft Learn and matches the documented lifecycle state as of the review date.

readBack.expectation: Attestation records that every cited source is published and consistent with its claim.

steps.intent: Inspect environment security roles and data loss prevention policy assignment relevant to Copilot Studio agents.
steps.expected: Assigned roles and DLP policy scope are visible for review; no role, license, or policy change is made.
steps.locator: Power Platform admin center > Policies > Data policies and (environment) > Security roles

readBack.expectation: Least-privilege role assignments and DLP scope are observed read-only, with no change applied.

phases.title: Remove local staging and record cleanup decision

steps.intent: Remove ignored local staging after required evidence has been captured by the lab result packager.
steps.expected: The Solution 23 lab-evidence staging directory no longer exists.
steps.command: $path = 'lab-evidence/23-copilot-studio-lifecycle-tracker'; if (Test-Path -LiteralPath $path) { Remove-Item -LiteralPath $path -Recurse -Force -ErrorAction Stop }; if (Test-Path -LiteralPath $path) { throw 'Failed to remove Solution 23 lab staging directory.' }

steps.intent: Capture operator attestation that no mutable changes were applied during this read-only run.
steps.expected: Operator note confirms cleanup is not required for this run.
steps.operatorNote: No mutation was executed in this read-only lifecycle validation run; only local documentation-first artifacts were produced.

readBack.expectation: Attestation confirms no tenant mutation occurred and only local sample artifacts were written.

requiredArtifacts.description: Redacted organization id/displayName response plus reference to the separately maintained sanctioned-lab authorization record.
PowerShell transcript and read-back output from the documentation-first monitor and evidence-export runs.
Screenshot of the Microsoft Agent 365 registry summary and Copilot Studio agent publish state captured read-only.
Source-citation record confirming each Microsoft Learn claim resolves and matches its documented lifecycle state.
